{{Quote|Unfortunately, Graf also tends to act prematurely. The clearest example of this occurred on Baes Logia.|Personal addendum by General Airen Cracken to an entry of a datafile detailing Rebel Alliance operatives dedicated to Graf Yonna|<ref name="CRO" />}}
'''Baes Logia''' was a terrestrial celestial body<ref name="CRO" /> situated in the [[Baes Logia system/Legends|Baes Logia system]], a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Quence sector/Legends|Quence sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> At one point during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Carosite]] [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Medic/Legends|medic]] [[Tamo Lan]] served in a [[Baes Logia campaign|short campaign]] on Baes Logia.<ref name="Journal10">{{JournalCite|10|Cracken's Rebel Operatives (article)}}</ref> During that same conflict, a trio of Rebel operatives once attempted to lure a [[Unidentified Imperial general (Baes Logia)|general]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] into a trap. The operatives acted as if they had been nearly overcome by [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial forces]]; however, as the [[General/Legends|general]] was approaching them, [[Graf Yonna]]—a [[Brain/Legends|mentally]] unstable Alliance operative who had partially assumed the personality of the ancient adventurer and hero [[Parsol d'Ahndole]]—also appeared. Yonna then opened [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]] fire, [[Death/Legends|killing]] the general. Not long after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Alliance General [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] noted the incident on Baes Logia in an entry dedicated to Yonna that was part of a [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] detailing various Rebel operatives.<ref name="CRO" />

==Behind the scenes==
Baes Logia was created by [[Chuck Truett]] and was introduced in ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', a [[1994]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook published for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="CRO" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Baes Logia [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the celestial body, in grid square O-18.<ref name="TEA" />
